pi_items.source: setup_invesalius.sh lines 71-73; DICOM dataset downloaded from https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip
Medical imaging literature confirms bone window uses W:2000 or W:3000 (https://litfl.com/abdominal-ct-windows-advanced/)
Medical imaging sources indicate bone window level ranges from 300-500 HU (https://pycad.co/window-width-vs-window-level-in-medical-imaging/)
Standard brain CT window is W:80, L:40 per multiple sources (https://myctregistryreview.com/courses/my-ct-registry-review-demo/lessons/ct-physics/topic/window-width-and-window-level/)
Standard brain CT window is W:80, L:40 (https://www.medmastery.com/guides/brain-ct-clinical-guide/how-optimize-display-brain-computed-tomography-ct-images)
Standard soft tissue window is 350/50 per radiology references (https://radiopaedia.org/articles/windowing-ct)
Bone window properties confirmed by radiology literature - displays bone as bright white with dark background
Brain window properties confirmed by radiology literature - shows brain tissue gray matter/white matter differentiation with saturated white skull
Soft tissue window properties confirmed - designed to show skin, scalp, and muscle details
setup_invesalius.sh line 73 contains explicit download URL
InVesalius 3.0 0051 sample dataset is a real CT cranium scan, not synthetic

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real medical imaging data from the official InVesalius 3.0 project's sample dataset (0051.zip), a CT cranium scan downloaded from https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip. All window/level settings are medically standard: Bone window (WW=2000, WL=300) matches orthopedic viewing standards, Brain window (WW=80, WL=40) is the standard for cranial CT evaluation, and Soft Tissue window (WW=350, WL=50) is the standard for soft tissue assessment. The visual descriptions match the expected characteristics of each windowing preset.
